verdict_reasoning: STIM1 (Stromal Interaction Molecule 1) is a single-pass type I transmembrane protein primarily resident in the ER membrane, where it senses luminal Ca2+ depletion via its EF-hand domain. Upon ER Ca2+ store depletion, STIM1 oligomerizes, translocates to ER-PM junctions, and physically couples with PM-resident ORAI channels. A small but documented pool of STIM1 also reaches the plasma membrane directly with its N-terminal EF-hand domain exposed extracellularly. This surface pool is upregulated in certain cell types and activation states. Because its PM presence is documented but not the dominant steady-state localization, this is best classified as contextual/dual_localization.
